title: How Small Teams Build a Reliable Podcast Production Pipeline
title_alternates: Why Podcast Production Breaks Even When the Recording Is Great
From Raw Recording to Publish: The Systems Behind a Great Podcast
description_short: Small podcast teams dont need more AIthey need a reliable production pipeline. Consultant Maya Chen joins us to break down how to go from raw recording to a consistent release bundle without burning out your team.
description_long:
Production systems consultant Maya Chen joins the show to map out a practical, reliable podcast production pipeline for small teams. We break the process into five stagescapture, post-production, content extraction, review and approval, and distributionand walk through where most shows get stuck.

Youll learn why the pipeline *is* the product, how to design a standard episode folder and output bundle, and why separating raw and cleaned transcripts is so important if you use automation. We also cover the limits of AI for titles and show notes, the highest-value checks before you publish, and how to start improving your workflow this month without adopting heavy tooling.

sponsor.name: SoundLayer
sponsor.description: A collaboration tool for small media teams that helps organize transcripts, asset versions, and review workflows.
sponsor.disclaimer: Mentioned in-episode as the sponsor; see link for product details.
